REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (15 Pinehurst Way, San Francisco, Ca 94127)
Off Calendar Mar-10-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Barbara deVries.

There being no overbid, the sale was confirmed at $759,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $721,050.00 is required.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And For Authority To Enter Into Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Feb-15-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara DeVries. Bill Stein for Admninstrator General of the State of Israel.

The Court approved the settlement agreement as proposed (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions To Initiate A Turnover Proceeding To Recover Trust Assets
Off Calendar Oct-26-2010 Continued To Nov-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle Lerman for Bud Izen. William Stein for The State of Isreal. Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara DeVries.

Mr. Stein stated that they did not have a settlement yet, that there was one aspect that needs to be cleared up, that he was hoping to hear from his Client soon and that it was more a matter of numbers than anything else. Ms. Lerman stated that Mr. Stein's Client had complete disregard of the Court's order to go to mediation. The Court referred the Parties to Judge Ross in Department 611 for a settlement conference. The matter was continued to Novmeber 16, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions To Initiate A Turnover Proceeding To Recover Trust Assets
Off Calendar Sep-28-2010 Continued To Oct-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bill Stein for the State of Israel.

Mr. Stein stated that they went through another mediation, that he sent the proposal to his Client and that he was waiting to hear back from them. The matter was continued to October 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation and for trial setting.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosada/A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing; (Status Re: Petition For Instructions)
Off Calendar Jun-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle Lerman for Bud Izen. Avid Alavi for Barbara DeVries (Temporary Trustee). Brook Brown on behalf of Bill Stein for The Administrator General of the State of Israel.

The Court inquired whether mediation was successful. Ms. Brown stated that there is a settlement, that they are awaiting approval, and that they anticipate a response soon. Ms. Lerman addressed the Court, stated that there is a total lack of communication by Mr. Stein, and requested that sanctions be imposed. The Court stated that it would not entertain an oral motion for sanctions. Ms. Lerman confirmed that her client was in agreement to the settlement which is currently being considered by the Administrator General. Ms. Alavi informed the Court that there is a home which there is a good market for and noted that as Trustee they weren't involved in the mediation. The Court stated that the matter would either be set for trial or it would hear the petition to approve the settlement. Ms. Lerman stated that it wouldn't need to be set for trial because the petition before the Court was to turn over the assets, that all of the assets have been turned over, and that they've got a complaint prepared to be filed. Ms. Brown stated that Mr. Stein had said that he expected to hear from his client by next week. The Court stated that the underlying petition would be continued, that the petition to approve the settlement will then be filed, that if the continuance date doesn't work for Mr. Stein he is to contact the Probate Examiner, Ms. Lerman, and Ms. Alavi. The matter was continued to July 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the filing of the petition for approval of the settlement or for trial setting. If the petition is on file then this matter will go off calendar.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing; (Status Re: Petition For Instructions)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle Lerman for Bud Izen. William Stein for The State of Isreal.

Counsel reported that all information had been provided, that the matter was progressing, that they were waiting for Mr. Stein's Client to review the information and that the Attorney for the Trustee had asked for approval to file an ex parte petition to sell the property. The Court aproved the request to file the ex parte petition. The matter was continued to June 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing; (Status Re: Petition For Instructions)
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Continued To Apr-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Stein for State of Israel. Michelle Lerman for Bud Izen.

Mr. Stein stated that they made progress at the last mediation, that they agreed to information being exchanged and that it was appropriate to have a second session of mediation. Ms. Lerman expressed concerns about the time period for which opposing Counsel was requesting information and opposing Counsel sticking to the original settlement amount. Mediation is to be completed by March 26, 2010 and status of mediation was set for April 6, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (order for the same signed in open Court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing; (Status Re: Petition For Instructions)
Off Calendar Dec-22-2009 Continued To Feb-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michell Lerman for Bud Eisen. Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara DeVries (Trustee). Bill Stein for the State of Israel.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Eisen's cooperation, the accounting, the $950,000.00 settlement amount and when Mr. Eisen was no longer in control of the assets. Mr. Siracusa stated that his Client would not consent to selling the San Francisco property without a settlement, that he received a wire transfer of $556,000.00 and that they had a property Manager handling the Tahoe property. The Court suggested Ms. Lerman provide the date of revocation of the Durable Power of Attorney, Mr. Stein outline further information needed to satisfy the question regarding expenses, decide on a settlement amount and decide if it is to be paid in cash or in payments. Mr. Stein's questions are due by January 5, 2010. Ms. Lerman's answers are due by January 15, 2010. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. Mediation is to be completed by February 19, 2010. A settlement conference is scheduled for February 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order referring the matter to mediation was signed in open Court.

Judge Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing; (Status Re: Petition For Instructions)
Off Calendar Dec-08-2009 Continued To Dec-22-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Barbara DeVries. Michelle Lerman for and with Bud Izan (Respondent). Bill Stein for The State of Isreal (Petitioner).

The Parties stated that there had been settlement talks, presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to co-mingling of assets and the Trustee providing an accounting. Mr. Izan agreed to write a check for $500,000.00 but Mr. Stein stated that they wanted to know where the funds went. Ms. Lerman stated that her Client was willing to put $577,000.00 less the property taxes and less the $6,700.00 outstanding bill back into the trust, and provide a declaration. Mr. Siracusa will file an ex parte petition to sell the property. The matter was continued to December 22, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For The Appointment Of A Trustee And For Instruction To The Trustee To Initiate A Turnover Proceeding To Recover The Trust'S Assets
Off Calendar Nov-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Gelb for The Administrator General Of The State Of Israel. Michelle Lerman for Bud Izen.

The Court inquired whether the issue of the third amendment could be worked out. Mr. Gelb stated that his client wants a Trustee in place and noted that they are close to settlement. Ms. Lerman stated that the Trust is a bogus Trust, that instead of using this hearing to determine if the Trust is valid the Court should support the settlement, and that the only thing that needs to take place is the sale of the San Francisco property. Mr. Gelb stated that Ms. Tashjian was clear that she wasn't leaving anything to Bud Izen, that Mr. Izen is holding the assets, that they don't know how much money is there, that they want a private fiduciary appointed, that the Trust assets aren't in one place, and that only Bud Izen knows what has been spent. Ms. Lerman stated that Bud is doing an excellent job, that Israel knows what's going on, that Israel received a copy of the 706, that the property in Tahoe has been leased, and that the last action is the sale of the San Francisco property. The Court inquired about appointing Mr. Izen with bond since the only issue is the sale of the real property. Mr. Gelb stated that the property isn't the only issue and that it would be safer to appoint a private fiduciary. Ms. Lerman stated that Mr. Izen was opposed to an appointment of a private fiduciary because of the cost. Discussions regarding the turnover were held. Additional arguments by both Counsel. The Court stated that it would appoint an independent professional and set status for December 8, 2009. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
